# servicenow_ui_policy (Data Source)

`servicenow_ui_policy` data source can be used to retrieve information of a single UI policy in ServiceNow by its short description.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Look up an existing UI policy in ServiceNow by its short description.
data "servicenow_ui_policy" "example" {
  short_description = "Make assignment_group mandatory when priority is critical"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `short_description` (String) Short description identifying this UI policy.

### Read-Only

- `active` (Boolean) Whether or not this UI policy is enabled.
- `conditions` (String) Encoded query conditions that trigger this UI policy.
- `description` (String) Description of what this UI policy does.
- `global` (Boolean) If true, the UI policy applies to all views of the table.
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `on_load` (Boolean) If true, the UI policy runs when the form loads.
- `order` (Number) The order in which this UI policy evaluates relative to others.
- `protection_policy` (String) Determines how application files are protected when downloaded or installed. Can be empty for no protection, 'read' for read-only protection or 'protected'.
- `reverse_if_false` (Boolean) If true, the actions are reversed when the condition is false.
- `run_scripts` (Boolean) If true, run the script_true and script_false scripts when the policy evaluates.
- `scope` (String) Associates a resource to a specific application ID in ServiceNow.
- `script_false` (String) Script to execute when the condition evaluates to false. ServiceNow seeds a default function body when the policy is created; specify a value here to override it.
- `script_true` (String) Script to execute when the condition evaluates to true. ServiceNow seeds a default function body when the policy is created; specify a value here to override it.
- `table` (String) Name of the table this UI policy applies to. Cannot be changed once created.
- `view` (String) Sys ID of the sys_ui_view this UI policy applies to.
